---
title: "VIGIAR Variable Dictionary"
author: "Ryan Santos"
date: "2026-06-20"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{VIGIAR Variable Dictionary}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>",
  eval = FALSE
)
```

## Dictionary

`vigiar` includes a package dictionary describing expected VIGIAR variables:

- original Power BI column name;
- standardized package column name;
- raw and processed type;
- description;
- unit;
- accepted values or plausible ranges;
- validation rule;
- notes.

```r
library(vigiar)

dict <- vigiar_dicionario()
head(dict)
```

## Structure

```r
names(dict)
```

Important columns include:

- `table_id`;
- `table_name`;
- `category`;
- `original_name`;
- `standard_name`;
- `type_raw`;
- `type_processed`;
- `description`;
- `unit`;
- `allowed_values`;
- `validation_rule`;
- `notes`.

## Domains

```r
vigiar_variaveis("pm25")
vigiar_variaveis("populacao_exposta")
vigiar_variaveis("indicadores_saude")
vigiar_variaveis("municipios")
```

## Specific Variables

```r
vigiar_descrever_variavel("pm25", "pm25_media_anual")
vigiar_descrever_variavel("indicadores_saude", "estimativa")
```

## Schema Views

```r
vigiar_schema("pm25")
vigiar_schema("exposicao_indoor")
```
